text: I'll get everything ready and meet you there.
Our new home. I'll see you there!
Goodbye, my love.
Some layabout named Sabjorn.
Been a thorn in my side for the last few years now.
Well, whoever this mysterious marking represents, they'll regret starting a war with me.
You should bring this information to the Thieves Guild immediately.
About time you showed up.
You might want to rethink that. You're missing out on some wonderful opportunities, working in that bar.
I'm not interested, Jaree. It doesn't matter how many times you ask.
Degaine the beggar got himself kicked out of the Temple of Dibella. Caused quite the ruckus.
Yes, I have something special for you.
But it's not for everyone to hear. Meet me in the Underforge tonight.
We will speak more.
Still eager to please. This is good. Skjor might be right about you.
He has something special planned for you. Better talk to him.
There you are.
Are you?
Well. I'm afraid I can't help you with that. I need the crystal to do anything useful, and I don't have it.
Hello, my love. Back from some adventure I bet.
*Sigh*... and that's the last one. All right, enough of this.
C'mon, let's get something to eat.
Here, this is fresh. I'll have another meal ready tomorrow.
Yes, my love? What do you need?
You have it. The Kel - the Elder Scroll. Tiid kreh... qalos. Time shudders at its touch.
There is no question. You are doom-driven. Kogaan Akatosh. The very bones of the earth are at your disposal.
Go then. Fulfill your destiny. Take the Scroll to the Time-Wound.
Do not delay. Alduin will be coming. He cannot miss the signs.
Use Dragonrend! It's the only chance to defeat him!
He is too strong on the wing! Bring him to gol with Dragonrend!
Use Dragonrend, Dovahkiin!
Dovahkiin, vosaraan! Use Dragonrend before Alduin consumes you!
Ruth wah nivahriin joor!
Aav uv dir. Join me or perish with your mortal friends.
Use Dragonrend! It is the only chance to defeat him!
He is too strong on the wing! Bring him to gol with Dragonrend!
Use Dragonrend, Dovahkiin!
Dovahkiin, vosaraan! Use Dragonrend before Alduin consumes you!
Now, Dovahkiin! Now he is vulnerable!
This is your chance, Dovahkiin! Strike with all your force!
Hurt him while he is grounded!
Kynesgrove is this way. We can travel together or split up and meet there, your choice.
That's better. Let's get on the road to Kynesgrove.
The Greybeards seem to think you're the Dragonborn. I hope they're right.
I think you're looking for this.
We need to talk.
When he shows up here and vouches for you, then I'll see what I can do for you.
If you really are Ralof's friend, I'll be glad to help you. But you didn't answer my question - how do you know him?
Here it is. Magnificent, isn't it?
Place the crystal in the central apparatus, and we can start the process for focusing it.
Took an incredible amount of work to get it running again. Now I'm hoping it'll all be worth it.
No one seems to know. Perhaps the return of the Dwemer. Perhaps the end of the world.
Waiting for what?
We're at war, old fool, or have you forgotten?
Whiterun is the crown jewel of Skyrim, and you would leave us defenseless?
As you command, my lord. But, the issue could be solved another way.
If we spend less on new arms and armor for the guards, for example...
Raise the taxes, then! Put anyone that refuses to pay in the stocks and make an example of them.
My lord Jarl, if we do not take steps soon, our coffers will be empty.
I'll be glad to help you. There are many ways to improve your home, provided you have the coin.
And of course, don't forget to consult your home decorating guide for descriptions of the decorations you can buy.
Now, what would you like to purchase?
You've already purchased every available improvement. I'm afraid I have nothing else to offer you.
Splendid! There's a house available right now.
The house I mentioned earlier is still available, if you want to buy it.
Wonderful! Here is the key to your new home.
Very well, just let me know when you're ready to purchase the house.
Very well, just let me know when you have the necessary funds.
Is there anything else you'd like to purchase?
As you wish. Speak to me again if you decide to purchase some decorations for your home.
I'm sorry, but you don't seem to have enough gold to pay for that.
Very well, I'll make the arrangements. The next time you visit your house, your new furnishings will be in place.
Hrongar became the Jarl when his brother Balgruuf, who had the job before him, was murdered.
Balgruuf was an even-tempered man, but his brother's a hot-blooded war-monger who wants to settle everything with a blade.
If you've got some business with Hrongar, mind you stay on his good side. The palace dungeon's filled with folk who roused his temper.
Vignar the Revered is the jarl in Whiterun. I guess he was a great hero once, because the Companions all call him "Vignar the Revered."
Is Vignar too old to be jarl? It's a fair question. If he's grown infirm and is losing his wits, where does that leave the people of Whiterun?
Lead us to your Sybil.
Dibella, guide us.
Come on. Hurry. We might be too late.
Wait. Something's wrong.
No, you don't want to go up there! A dragon... it's attacking!
The memories. I cannot stand them. You must take them away, return them to Avanchnzel.
You must take the Lexicon from me. Please... take it now.
Ha ha! I'd rather have two bottles of mead than two wenches, friend.
You've never had a taste of sweet Honningbrew? Why, it's only the finest mead outside of Sovngarde!
Drink Honningbrew Mead with a wench on each arm? Ha ha!
Black-Briar? How dare you shove that swill in my face. I'd rather drink mammoth drool!
Ah, but you look tired. Come, share a bottle of Honningbrew Mead with me!
Ah, is there any way you'd rather spend the day than drinking Honningbrew Mead?
Cheers, my friend! May your adventures find you fame and fortune!
Hail, friend! It's good to see another merry soul enjoying this fine day.
I don't talk to people who drink that Black-Briar drench.
This good deed should not go unrewarded. Here, take this!
Ha! Too true, my friend. Too true...
Ysmir's beard! You're one after my own heart. I would love another bottle.
Ah, nothing like fine spirits to help raise your... well, your spirits!
The meads won't argue your ear off about which one should go first!
Black-Briar Mead? What are you trying to do? Poison me?
But enough talk. Come! Won't you share a drink with me and my companions?
Why's that?
Its sweetness is like a fair maiden's kiss on a starry night. And it's got enough kick to put a frost troll on his hind quarters! Ha!
Poisons can kill your enemies faster than any blade.
I am wise woman to this stronghold.
We can trade in the morning, outlander.
One day, I must fight my brother Nagrub for chiefdom. It will not be easy.
It's late outlander. Come back in the morning if you need to spend coin.
We only trade during the day, outlander. At night, most rest in the longhouse.
I'm here to mine, outlander. So unless you want to dig, get out.
I have metal to dig.
Malacath has given me the task to mine. Good ore brings strength to our people.
Every Orc learns the heavy labor of mining. All spend years in the caves.
The mines are hard, but Malacath rewards those who rise to the challenge.
Come on in. Just stoked the fire. Take a seat and get the cold out.
Can I help you?
Oh, do come in! I hope I have what you need.
Not in our nature.
You don't need my fruit, I've heard when you sing people throw tomatoes at you anyway.
I think I may wait until the Burning of King Olaf to have a Spiced Wine.
You bards don't cut each other any slack do you?
How about I trade you a song for some fruit?
Positions.
It is a shame, though.
Speaking of harsh.
Perhaps if I were a little more blessed I could afford to give away my fruit. As is you'll have to pay, Rorlund.
Perhaps you could spare a tomato for a priest of the Divines?
It's too bad the Burning of King Olaf was called off this year. I think some of the younger bards would have really gotten a kick out of it.
I think our flutist here has gotten better since the Burning of King Olaf.
Lock the city gate.
That's wonderful. You've done us a great service. As promised the outfit is yours and here's something for your trouble.
He's okay. A little breathy and a little harsh on the notes.
Take a good look around. I'm sure you'll find what you're looking for. If not, let me know, I might have it stored away.
Got something for just about everybody in here. Give a holler if you have any questions.
Aren't you a sight for sore eyes.
Hello friend. Make yourself at home.
You lost?
I trust you're not planning any trouble. What can I do for you, friend?
If you've got the coin, you've come to the right place. Pull up a seat.
Welcome. Let me know if you want anything... think I got a clean mug around here somewhere.
Come on in. We got warm food, warm drinks, and warm beds.
She's okay. A little breathy and a little harsh on the notes.
My mother always used to say that too.
It is. We train the bards you hear throughout the land. Viarmo is the head of the college and in charge of admissions.
Only in that I'm a graduate. I work here at the Winking Skeever. I would highly recommend it, though. Talk to Viarmo, if you're interested.
Only in that I'm a graduate. I work over at the Winking Skeever. I would highly recommend it, though. Talk to Viarmo, if you're interested.
Oh really? Well you can tell them that I will be putting in a request for a few dresses quite soon.
Lurbuk?
Fancies himself a bard. He pays, so I let him stay.
If I had any customers, I'd be worried about him annoying them. But, well... Look around.
No, no. Well, sometimes, but that's not the problem.
I just often don't have much to do, so I tend to... try things out. They don't usually work, but I try.
Next thing I know, I've used up most of my stock.
The marsh is treacherous. You would do well to not wander at night.
As anyone will tell you, Morthal can be a dangerous place.
My... talents... are useful here. I help maintain order, even if it goes unnoticed.
Slow? No. It just ain't there at all.
Few enough reasons to pass through Morthal before the war started.
Now... Well, let's just say the front door doesn't get much use.
Fine, Ataf. You should eat some more tomatoes, though.
It's quite fetching actually. The craftsmanship is excellent.
I could listen to him play all day.
I could listen to her play all day.
Um, excuse me. Could I buy two leeks?
Well, you can understand Elisif not wanting it.
I'm so glad we got to have it. I thought for sure Elisif would never consent.
Look silly! As if you could look any sillier than you do now.
I'm afraid that kind of thing doesn't work on captains in the Imperial Army.
Generally, if you want rumors, you should talk to Corpulus. That said... I did see a visitor pass through on his way to the Blue Palace.
Absolutely not. I prefer to be near enough to come visit, but free to come and go as I please.
Good. Here's the outfit. Try to get Elisif's opinion on it and if she likes it mention it's from Radiant Raiment.
It's those damned Blackblood Marauders. They're scaring off shipping.
One apple. Since the Blackbloods were dispatched I've had good luck getting shipments.
Oh, no. But being surrounded by good music, good drink and good friends is all I've ever asked for.
Do you miss your days training at the College?
If you're heading to the Blue Palace, you might want to rethink that outfit.
Hmm... If you ever want to replace those old rags, stop by Radiant Raiment.
Youngest bard at the Bards College, at your service.
Anything I can do for you, I will.
The pleasure's all mine.
So exciting when new folk come into town. I bet you've had plenty of adventures.
Someday, I'll be a gallant heroine. You make it look so... appealing.
Maybe you need a partner? Hmm? Just picture it. Two women, the dusty road, strapping men at every tavern, a fortune in gold to be claimed...
You're really going to the Blue Palace? That presents an opportunity.
If you are willing to wear one of Radiant Raiment's outfits and speak to the jarl, I would not only pay you but let you keep the outfit.
Your fruit are looking a little less ripe today.
Your selection looks very delicious. I'll take an apple.
Ah, you and everyone else. Word is it may not happen this year though.
Damn shame, Roggvir. You were a good man.
That makes twenty-three.
Talos protect you.
Roggvir. You damn fool.
She did, did she? She's going to get a sit down later.
Yeah, that's what Papa says too.
You would do that? That's great. You should go do it right now.
Thanks. Papa talks about it some. Mum doesn't say much since Uncle Roggvir died. She doesn't even go to temple anymore.
I wish she would. At least that way she'd have people to talk to.
Thanks so much. I'll return to temple. Here's something for your time and effort.
Why did you... nevermind, it's a sign. I'll return to temple. Here's something for your help.
Thanks.
Well, I wish it were that simple.
Maybe it is. Look, I want to go back but I won't feel right in there without a tie to Talos.
My brother Roggvir wore a symbol of Talos. He kept it hidden on his person. If I had that...
I can't go near his body. Would you get it back for me?
Free of charge for you.
Hello, Silana. Would you like an apple?
I'm pleased when the palace is clean, I'm pleased when the beds are made on time, I'm pleased when Erdi isn't around.
You're a hard woman to please, Una.
You don't put up with a man like that. He puts up with you.
Vegetables are the food of the Divines.
There's nothing quite like a good vegetable.
I once saw Fihada put an arrow in between the eyes of a Blackblood Marauder at five hundred paces.
I keep coming out here thinking I'll see what others see in this... frivolity.
How do you put up with that fop Fihada?
I've heard stranger things are eaten over at the Temple of the Divines.
Most of those don't happen very often.
I've tasted your food, Odar. It's better than most songs I've heard.
Well, you never can tell.
Sometimes I like to think my cooking is like playing the flute or singing a song.
The guy in from Dragon Bridge? He didn't seem the type.
I don't think that was a compliment.
I'll need a whole crate of fruit. That Varnius Junius went through quite a few of our supplies.
You like everyone, Jorn. You'll make a good court bard some day.
That's generous, Jala, but I am happy to pay my way.
I like you, Una. You've got presence.
By the gods! What manner of power is that?
That is one... big hammer.
Your shield... Dwarf-make, is it not? But yet it seems so much... more.
What a foul and unnatural weapon you wield. Never have I seen its like.
What manner of staff is that? Such a horror...
You wield... Azura's Star? How... how can such a thing be possible?
Fine armor you've got there. Dwarven make, am I right?
What do you aim to do with that hammer, friend? Knock down a house?
No... get that accursed mace away from me...
Still can't wake him up? Perhaps you're barking up the wrong tree?
You seem to be having a small problem... or perhaps it's a big problem? Maybe if you shrunk the whole thing down a little first?
Hmm... Your creature doesn't appear to be fairing any better than before. Methinks your aim is off.
Dammit...
I think this might be the end...
Been about eight years now. I used to be a mercenary, but one trip to Ravenscar Hollow cured me of that.
There's hagravens in that cave with talons like daggers. They'll open your bowels quick as you wink if you let 'em get close.
Creature like that'll make a man think long and hard about what he wants out of life.
My pa used to say there ain't no use worryin' about the things you can't change.
If it's my fate to be ended by a dragon, so be it. Meantime, I'll live my life the way I want to.
Enchanting services are one of the few things that keep us in touch with the rest of Skyrim.
Perhaps we can go another few decades without a disaster. That would be nice.
I'm working on some very important research, I assure you.
The Eye of Magnus. Here, but lost. Slipped through our grasp. Oh, what we might have learned.
I wonder what we could've done with the Eye, had it been put to better use?
Have you seen Mirabelle around? I know she's dead, but I thought... Well, I thought she might stop in and say hello anyway.
You know, they say knowledge is its own reward.
Arch-Mage or not, my rules about books still stand.
All this knowledge might have been lost, were it not for you.
I don't want to see you treating any of these books poorly. Are we clear?
Tolfdir is master wizard. Certainly didn't anticipate that.
And to think, Mirabelle questioned my allowing you into the College. I can just imagine the look on her face now.
Mirabelle does a fine job as master wizard, I suppose. Still, there's always room for improvement.
I care little for idle conversation, but perhaps you need help with Conjuration magic?
Ancano is exactly why we're so restrictive about access to the College. We'd all have perished if it weren't for you.
I'm sure you've made note of who's been the most helpful during your time here, Arch-Mage.
May I just say, it's a pleasure to have you leading the College. Really.
If there's ever any way I can assist you, please let me know.
I'm quite pleased that we're back to business as usual.
Certainly nothing illicit going on here, Arch-Mage. You can be quite sure of that.
Even with the College's laid-back attitude, there are still some things that are off-limits.
Like it or not, you are a shining example to future generations of College mages.
After all that it's been through, the College still stands. In no small part thanks to you!
I don't know what it's doing up there, but I'm not waiting around to find out!
It flew over the town and landed on the old dragon burial mound.
Well, I don't know. Not yet... it flew over the town and landed on the old dragon mound!
It's always encouraging to see the younger generations embracing education.
I still can't believe it. You, the Arch-Mage? Never would have guessed it.
I never would have suspected that the Eye could be so dangerous. Tolfdir is right, isn't he? Magic really can overwhelm you.
I've certainly learned more here than I ever expected to.
I wonder if the College's reputation will ever recover from our adventures.
The College is a bit of an oddity in Skyrim. Magic isn't something that's looked upon fondly here.
Back in 42, I was stationed in Hammerfell, on leave in Sentinel, trying to track down some refugee relatives who had fled persecution in Alinor.
Suddenly an explosion of magic in the refugee quarter. Thalmor mages were attacking the Altmer dissidents who were resisting with magic of their own.
I ran to the scene with other Legionnaires who were stationed there, but the entire quarter was a smoking ruin by the time we arrived.
Everyone was dead. Wholesale slaughter. The Dominion, not content with killing dissidents at home, came to Hammerfell to finish the job.
We're supposedly at peace now. But I put in to be stationed here to keep an eye on the Thalmor. I've a feeling they're behind this unrest in Skyrim.
My parents were traveling merchants eventually settling in Cyrodiil. I must have inherited their wanderlust...
I joined the Legion to see the world. I know, that's one of those things people say, but it's completely true in my case.
Wasn't long before I saw more of the world than I bargained for.
For the glory of the Empire.
The life of a soldier is full of hardship. That's nothing.
But they send the Legion to places that've gotten too bad to be settled without violence. What's hard is seeing good people warped by evil.
And I've seen the face of evil. It was in the air above Sentinel on the Night of Green Fire.
Home? Home to me is a hot cup of ale at the end of the day and five minutes without someone needing something from me...
Cyrodiil is a beautiful place, full of diverse peoples and histories. But so too, Hammerfell and Skyrim, and every other place I've been.
Don't get me wrong, every country has its dark corners. But that's why we're here. To bring order and civilization, and to protect the people.
Ulfric and his thugs are stirring up trouble for their own agenda. The Empire is the only thing keeping the Dominion from walking all over Skyrim.
It matters little if some of the people here are ignorant to that truth. They are still citizens of the Empire. It's our solemn duty to protect them.
Aye. I've worked on a few moorings here and there for it. Most of the ship captains are superstitious about who works on their vessels, though.
And Dawnstar is known for its bad luck. We're the last port of call before Windhelm, and the ore we trade makes rich targets for pirates.
Pfft. I'm the oldest woman in Dawnstar. Was here when Skald the Elder was Skald the Younger.
He's a fool, if you haven't met him already. Thinks Ulfric Stormcloak is invincible and spits dragon fire.
The people here look to Brina Merilis when they need things settled. Real firebrand going up. Wasn't surprised when she joined the Legion.
That's right. In the Second Era, Tiber Septim himself led the army that conquered Old Hroldan from the barbarians of The Reach.
Septim would later found the Empire that united Tamriel, but his first known battle and victory was right here.
And this inn has the very bed the great general slept in on his first night as Old Hroldan's liberator. As good as it was hundreds of years ago.
Every Nord should admire Ulfric. He fights for all of us.
In fact, he's the reason I chose to learn the smithing trade. I want to make weapons and armor for the great Stormcloak army.
Grew up here. Cold as Atmora, but that just grows the beards thicker!
Lot of history in these walls. We're trying to make some more. It's a lucky time to be alive.
Wherever I can find them. If you're the type who worries over where something came from, you may want to move on.
Are you kidding? I came all the way from Cyrodiil just to work with him.
He's... he's not the best teacher. But just watching him, I've learned so much.
I don't see these troubles as business. This is a labor of honor.
To bring Ulfric his rightful glory, I would hammer a thousand shields.
Shor's bones, I might just have to!
No, but then I don't have much need for Ulfric.
We both leave well enough alone.
Smithing isn't the most exciting thing for me, but we all have to lend a hand as best we can.
If a Stormcloak uses one of my swords to strike down an Imperial soldier, then I've done my part to free our land.
It doesn't matter what I believe. Nurelion does, and it might be the only thing keeping him alive right now.
The Silver Hand has been scouring the world for more pieces of Wuuthrad.
The Silver Hand has been scouring the world for more pieces of Wuuthrad.
Another piece of glory. Good work, Shield-Brother.
Another piece of glory. Good work, Shield-Sister.
Whatever I don't already know.
Power is the crux of this world. There's always more of it to be had, if you look in the right places.
We work where we can get work. The Nords don't like unloading their ships, so we do it instead.
Sometimes cargo goes missing.
Every bit as sharp as what you'll find in Whiterun.
I respect Eorlund, but he has the good fortune to work the Skyforge. Something about the fires... their steel just holds tighter.
Get it back, for the honor of us all.
There's an old saying that only a Nord can work the soil in Skyrim. I don't think anyone else would have the patience for it.
It takes half a day just to dig a planting row in the cold, stony earth here. But what else can we do, eat rocks and ice?
I'll carry on his tradition as best I can. I could spend a lifetime just going over his notes.
I came here as a hatchling. The only thing colder than Skyrim is the Nords themselves, but we stick together.
You know what I mean. Forges have personalities, right?
If that makes any sense.
Just got here from the Summerset Isles. Lots of opportunity in Skyrim.
For Ulfric? Oh, nothing official. Known him for years.
He seems to value my thoughts, and I'm grateful for that.
I don't really have a mind for war, and I think he likes having an untrained opinion from time to time.
Did you see the candle above the fire upstairs?
It was lit one hundred and sixty-three years ago, back when this building was the home of a grand warrior named Vundheim.
When word came that he'd fallen, his son Deroct lit the candle in his honor. Nobody knows why it still burns.
One group has hidden out in The Reach.
One group has hidden out in Haafingar.
One group has hidden out in Hjaalmarch.
One group has hidden out in Falkreath.
One group has hidden out in Whiterun.
One group has hidden out in The Pale.
One group has hidden out in The Rift.
One group has hidden out in Eastmarch.
One group has hidden out in Winterhold.
J'zargo had expected to be named Arch-Mage. But there is still time.
Are the Arch-Mage's robes as comfortable as J'zargo believes them to be?
Skyrim could not be more different from Elsweyr. But magic... Magic is the same no matter where you go.
Greetings, Arch-Mage.
May I help you, Arch-Mage?
Then stop wasting my time.
Have you found proof of Alva's treachery?
You better get proof of your accusations against Alva.
That's right. Home of the Redguards. The land of sand and seas.
I hope one day I'll convince Rustleif to take us back there. Until then, I'm content running the forge together here in Dawnstar.
Not much. I came here a few years ago from Hammerfell when I married.
The land is beautiful, but I can't stand this cold. I don't know how Rustleif manages it.
Come back if you need steel.
We forge every day if you need anything.
The only thing I dislike about my husband's homeland is the cold. Why is it always so cold?
Sahloknir! Ziil gro dovah ulse!
Sahloknir! Ziil gro dovah ulse!
Come on. We need to find out what's happening up there!
I'm not going out there. Not with that dragon on the loose.
I wouldn't go out there for all the money in the world.
Do you think it's gone yet?
We've got to find someplace to hide!
Ful, losei Dovahkiin? Zu'u koraav nid nol dov do hi.
Sahloknir, krii daar joorre.
Don't you know there's a dragon attacking?
You do not even know our tongue, do you? Such arrogance, to dare take for yourself the name of Dovah.
There you go!
Pretty good arm you have there.
How's the newcomer look?
He might just make it.
She might just make it.
Looks slow to me.
I could take him.
I could take her.
Not bad.
Come on, now.
Come at me. You couldn't hurt me if you wanted to.
Don't just stand there.
What are you waiting for?
Here, you should have this.
Oh, I'm sorry about that.
I want to thank you for your help. I know my master can be a bit short at times.
Sir.
Get those documents to Whiterun.
To Whiterun with you.
Thank you, Legate. In the future, I'll let you know when your opinion is needed.
You have your orders, soldier.
I thought I gave you something to do?
You don't think this is a war already? Sir, I...
Don't keep waiting, soldier.
Good luck with that ice wraith.
We have it on good authority that Ulfric has raised enough men to attack the city of Whiterun. The jarl, however, refuses the Legion's support.
This missive should convince him. Be aware, soldier, these documents contain sensitive intelligence for the jarl's eyes only.
Yes. An axe. How long have you been in Skyrim? Give the man my axe.
If he keeps it, I will bide my time. If he returns it to you, it means war.
Men who understand each other often have no need for words.
There are but a few simple truths behind one warrior giving another his axe.
Balgruuf will know my meaning.
Men who understand each other often have no need for words.
There are but a few simple truths behind one warrior giving another his axe.
Hrongar will know my meaning.
Keep your wits about you. The Jarl of Whiterun is known for his temper.
What? Get over with the rest of the men at Korvanjund.
Korvanjund. Remember?
I can take a hint, lad. You want to make some coin, come find me.
I can take a hint, lass. You want to make some coin, come find me.
It's our name for the old sewers that run under the city.
It used to be a huge system hundreds of years ago, but it fell into decay, just like the rest of Riften.
The Guild stocks the place full of thugs that aren't good enough to join, so watch yourself if you venture down there.
They'd stop at nothing to take your last bit of food if that's all you had.
The Ratway's too good a place for that scum.
They used to be pretty feared around here. I mean, you'd whisper the name and it'd send chills down your spine.
Now, they're nothing more than ruffians and thugs trying to pry a few extra coin from honest people.
All it would take is a small force of guard to go into the Ratway and flush them out.
Get moving, now. I'll be fine.
Come on, get up... I need you on your feet.
Do we have to? They'd never lift a finger to help us.
We'll stay here. Get back to the College and let them know what's going on.
